tours.name: Emergency Department
tours.summary:
Queensway Carleton Hospital’s new Emergency Department expansion is being built to meet the urgent realities of Ottawa’s rapidly growing west end. The hospital was originally built in 1976 for a community of about 100,000 people, but it now serves more than 500,000 residents and remains the only full-service hospital in the city’s west end.

Today, the Emergency Department is operating far beyond its intended capacity. Designed for 63,000 visits a year, it now sees more than 83,000 visits annually and is trending toward 100,000, creating longer waits and greater strain on patients, families, and frontline teams.

This once-in-a-generation expansion will more than double the size of the Emergency Department, add 90 urgently needed inpatient beds, and bring critical diagnostic imaging such as CT and X-ray directly into the department. It will also include a dedicated mental health care space with private treatment rooms, controlled lighting, reduced noise, and a separate entrance to provide safer, more dignified care for patients in crisis.

The goal is simple: faster diagnosis, shorter wait times, improved patient flow, and better care when every minute matters. This is not just a construction project — it is a vital investment in readiness, safety, and care for families across Ottawa’s west end.
